sections.biography-source-undefined:
Jiang Wan, styled Gongyan, was from Xiangxiang county, Lingling commandery.

In the ninth year of the Yanxi era, 246, he died from his illness in Fu county. He was buried there as it was claimed to be an auspicious place by diviners.

full_text:
Jiang Wan (Gongyan) 蔣琬 (公琰)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Jiang Wan (蔣琬), styledGongyan (公琰), was born in Xiangxiang county, Lingling commandery. Trusted and influential within the Shu court, he led it for Liu Shan. Jiang Wan was affiliated with the Shu Kingdom. Jiang Wan lived during the Three Kingdoms periods of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Jiang Wan, styled Gongyan, was from Xiangxiang county, Lingling commandery.
In the ninth year of the Yanxi era, 246, he died from his illness in Fu county. He was buried there as it was claimed to be an auspicious place by diviners.
